'''Thomas "Tom" Minns''' (born 4 September 1994) is an English former [[rugby league]] and current [[rugby union]] player who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for [[Hull RUFC]] in the [[National League 1]].

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]], and on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the [[London Broncos]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]], and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in two separate spells in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Minns was born in [[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.

He grew up in seacroft and attended David young community academy. He was head boy

==Playing career==

===Leeds Rhinos===
In 2013, Minns made three appearances for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and scored a single try.<ref name="loverugbyleague">{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Thomas-Minns|title=Thomas Minns Player Stats|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=2014-05-28}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
In 2014, he was loaned to the [[London Broncos]], with two of his fellow, young team-mates from Leeds.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===Featherstone Rovers===
Minns spent the 2015 season on loan at [[Featherstone Rovers]], recording 16 outings and scoring eight tries.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 3 July 2015, it was announced that Minns had signed a three-year deal to play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]], starting from the 2016 [[rugby league]] season. Minns suffered relegation from the [[Super League]] with Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2016 season, due to losing the [[Million Pound Game]] at the hands of [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]. 12-months later however, Minns was part of Hull Kingston Rovers side that won promotion back to the [[Super League]], at the first time of asking following relegation the season prior. On 22 May 2019, it was announced that Minns would be returning to play for Hull Kingston Rovers until the end of the 2019 season.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 25 October 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Newcastle Thunder coach Eamon O'Carroll excited by quartet of signings|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/newcastle-thunder-eamon-ocarroll-excited-signings/}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 5 January 2022, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Jan 2022|accessdate=15 Jan 2022|title=Thomas Minns "buzzing" to be at Wakefield|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/}}</ref>

===Goole Vikings===
On 4 Oct 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL League 1]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=4 Oct 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Goole Vikings land their fifth signing with ex-Super League man|url=https://www.totalrl.com/goole-vikings-land-their-fifth-signing-with-ex-super-league-man/}}</ref>

===Hull RUFC===
On 11 September 2025 it was reported that he had switched codes to play [[rugby union]] for [[Hull RUFC]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Sep 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Hull KR star seals rugby union switch following League 1 exit|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-hull-kr-star-thomas-minns-seals-rugby-union-switch}}</ref>
